decisions.title: Adopt AI-native development workflow as engineering standard
decisions.context: Multiple signals indicate engineering exploring AI tooling (ENG-9327 spec-driven dev, ENG-9328 property-based tests, ENG-9325 knowledge graph, ENG-9324 code review tools, ENG-9316 unified config). Need decision on standardization vs experimentation phase.

arguments.for: Aligns with 'Build the Moat' pillar - could 2x engineering velocity. Multiple teams independently exploring suggests organic demand. Sift Edge and Ask SIFT products prove AI capability.
arguments.against: Premature standardization could stifle innovation. Security/compliance concerns with AI code access (see ENG-9312 audit trail). Tool landscape still evolving rapidly.

decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas metadata completion for Q2 launch
decisions.context: ENG-9309 shows Canvas metadata work in progress. Canvas is key 2026 product for 'Embed Deeper' pillar but metadata foundation critical for decision capture.

arguments.for: Canvas directly drives North Star metric (Decisions Captured). Metadata enables versioning, audit trail, and AI context. Dependencies blocking other Canvas features.
arguments.against: Explore V2 may need resources. Metadata can be added incrementally post-launch if core collaboration works.

decisions.title: Resolve Parquet schema design to unblock data ingestion
decisions.context: ENG-9255 design work + ENG-9152 channel metadata ingestion issue suggests data pipeline blocker. Impacts ability to 'Nail the Core' if historical decision data not accessible.
decisions.blockedBy: Schema design review pending architecture approval

arguments.for: Parquet enables efficient storage for decision history at scale. Channel metadata critical for multi-workspace customers (embed deeper use case).
arguments.against: Could ship with simpler JSON storage initially. Parquet optimization premature if customer volume low.

decisions.title: Security posture: Address CVEs vs new security automation
decisions.context: Three CVEs flagged (ENG-8398 apache-avro RCE, ENG-8406 zookeeper, ENG-8408 crypto/tls) while simultaneously investing in agent audit trails (ENG-9312) and incident automation (ENG-9290). Resource allocation question.

arguments.for: Automation investments prevent future incidents at scale. Agent audit trails enable safer AI adoption (strategic moat). CVEs may have workarounds.
arguments.against: Known RCE vulnerability is customer trust risk. Can't build moat if core security broken. Automation can wait 1 quarter.

decisions.title: Customer-specific UI generation agent: Build vs partner
decisions.context: ENG-9332 suggests building custom UI generation. High complexity, uncertain ROI vs North Star metric. Could distract from core products.

arguments.for: Ultimate 'embed deeper' - UI adapts per customer workflow. Differentiated capability competitors can't match. Leverages Sift Edge infrastructure.
arguments.against: Scope creep from North Star (decisions captured, not UI customization). High engineering cost. Third-party tools (Retool, etc) may suffice.

thisWeek.breakdown: 8 roadmap/planning, 6 incident/ops, 5 customer escalations, 3 team syncs

timeAudit.weekSummary: High interrupt-driven load from security CVEs and parquet ingestion issues. Only 20% time on strategic initiatives (Canvas, AI tooling). Need to batch incident response.
alerts: Zero exec time allocated to 'Build the Moat' pillar this week despite 7 AI-related eng tickets
Parquet issue has consumed 6 eng hours without clear resolution path - escalate decision
Customer-specific UI agent (ENG-9332) initiated without product brief or success metrics
Three critical CVEs unaddressed beyond initial triage - security SLA breach risk
